title: rename `--group-add=keep-groups` to `--keep-groups`
body:
### Feature request description

In a very narrow edge case where `keep-groups` is itself a group name, it cannot be added with `--group-add`.

### Suggest potential solution

Replace `--group-add=keep-groups` with a separate `--keep-groups` option.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I'm not aware of any.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
Is that actually a practical concern, i.e. do you actual use a group with the name `keep-groups`?

While we can add a new option, we cannot remove the special meaning of `--group-add=keep-groups` as this will break any user of that option so we cannot do that without a major release and even then there must be a strong reason for it.

it's just an easy change to avoid a magic value where there's no need for one. I don't need it urgently, but it could be deprecated with a warning message to prevent an unexpected break as you describe. i think it's worth considering from a cmd pattern perspective.

> it's just an easy change to avoid a magic value where there's no need for one

The decision is whether it is worth to break existing users for a theoretical issue.  `keep-groups` seems like a quite weird identifier for a group, and you could still specify its numerical value if it turns out to be necessary
(and I agree with you that using a special meaning for `keep-groups` was an unfortunate choice)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
